sections.heading: DIFFWAVE: A VERSATILE DIFFUSION MODEL FOR AUDIO SYNTHESIS

figures.label: Figure 1
figures.caption: Figure 1: The diffusion and reverse process in diffusion probabilistic models. The reverse process gradually converts the white noise signal into speech waveform through a Markov chain $p _ { \theta } ( x _ { t - 1 } | x _ { t } )$ .

figures.label: Figure 2
figures.caption: Figure 2: The network architecture of DiffWave in modeling $\epsilon _ { \theta } : \mathbb { R } ^ { L } \times \mathbb { N } \to \mathbb { R } ^ { L }$ .

tables.label: Table 3
tables.caption: Table 3: The automatic evaluation metrics (Accuracy, FID-class, IS, mIS), and 5-scale MOS with $9 5 \%$ confidence intervals for WaveNet and DiffWave on the class-conditional generation task.

figures.label: Figure 3
figures.caption: Figure 3: The network architecture of DiffWave in modeling $\epsilon _ { \theta } ( x _ { t } , t )$ , including tensor shapes at each stage and activation functions. $B$ is the batch size, $C$ is the number of residual/skip channels of the network, and $L$ is data dimension.
